title: Corybas arachnoideus
taxonomy: Corybas arachnoideus is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This species belongs to the genus Corybas, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. Specifically, Corybas arachnoideus falls under the subtribe Zebrinae, a group of orchids characterized by their unique flowers and growth habits. The subtribe Zebrinae is further divided into several genera, with Corybas being one of them. The exact classification of Corybas arachnoideus is as follows: Corybas arachnoideus (R.Br.) R.Br., family Orchidaceae, subtribe Zebrinae, genus Corybas.
uses: Corybas arachnoideus, also known as the spider orchid, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. In some cultures, the plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat sore throats and other respiratory issues. Its extracts are also thought to possess antimicrobial properties, which could aid in wound healing.
